questions.question: What is the basic functional, filtering unit of the human kidney?
Which hormone, produced by the beta cells of the pancreas, acts to lower blood glucose levels?
In the human circulatory system, which specific blood vessels carry oxygenated blood away from the lungs back to the left atrium of the heart?
What gap or junction allows a chemical or electrical signal to pass from one neuron terminal to the next receiving neuron?
Which section of the human brain is primarily responsible for coordinating voluntary movements, balance, and posture?
What type of blood cells are vital components of the immune system and are responsible for fighting off infections?
What is the primary site of nutrient absorption within the human digestive system?
Which protein molecule gives red blood cells their color and binds reversibly to oxygen gas?
What mechanism maintains internal stability and balance within a biological organism in response to external environmental changes?
Which endocrine gland is frequently referred to as the 'master gland' because its hormones regulate many other endocrine glands?
